mnemonics: D.I.S.G.L. (Dance In Style, Get Loaded) - Describes the 5 key servlet lifecycle methods: destroy(), init(), service(), doGet(), doLoad() (though doLoad isn't standard, it helps remember doGet/doPost for handling requests)
Servlet Life Cycle Order: I S D (Init, Service, Destroy) - Like 'I See Destruction' of requests
keyPoints: Servlet is initialized once via init() method when first requested
service() method handles each client request by calling appropriate doGet()/doPost()
Servlet is destroyed once via destroy() method when container takes it out of service
The web container (like Tomcat) manages the entire lifecycle
init() and destroy() are called only once during servlet's lifetime
commonMistakes: Assuming init() is called for every request (it's called only once)
Forgetting to call super.init(config) when overriding init() method
Not distinguishing between service() method and doGet()/doPost() methods
Implementing servlet destruction logic in finalize() instead of destroy() method
